entries.name: The Toshi Ranbo front — the Lion–Crane war
entries.note: PRIMARY. The Lion and Crane — ancestral enemies — are at open war over the contested town of Toshi Ranbo (Akodo Arasou slain by Doji Hotaru's arrow, 1123). Akihiko's Legion is deployed to enforce an Imperial edict between two armies that hate each other. (facts.py relation lion crane)

entries.name: Keep my soldiers alive — ninjō vs the orders that spend them
entries.note: The engine of the drama. His cohort carries a FALCON (Toritaka) contingent — clansmen serving the Legion under their famous-blooded son; his ninjō is to march them home to the Valley of the Spirits ALIVE. Every order that spends lives to win the Emperor's war pokes it. The cruelty: his giri (honor the Falcon) is paid in the same Falcon lives his ninjō would save. Trigger to watch: Softheartedness (Fire) — he seizes up about KILLING, +3 strife when he takes a life. (Also Fear of Mediocrity (Fire): +3 strife when he must improvise/adapt to the unexpected on a Fire check.)

entries.name: The black-sheep blood & uncle Seppun Tadanobu's leash
entries.note: His known Imperial blood comes from the scandalous branch — grandfather left the Seppun to marry a Toritaka; uncle Tadanobu redeemed it and sponsors Akihiko. The favor is a leash: what does the uncle expect in return, and who resents the upjumped black-sheep nephew?

entries.name: Officers who know whose nephew he is — expectation & resentment
entries.note: Akihiko serves under officers aware of his Seppun patron and his minor-clan origins. ELEVATION here is rolled, not narrated (Status-priced Fate Questions); trust and favor arrive with strings.

entries.name: The spirit-dark of the battlefield — what the Falcon sees that the war hides
entries.note: Quiet thread. A battlefield is a feast for the dead; Akihiko's Falcon spirit-sight (Sixth Sense) may find yūrei, gaki, or worse moving under cover of the mundane war. Latent — let the dice surface it.

entries.name: A Lion's debt — the life I owe Matsu Kasumi
entries.note: Akihiko lives because Matsu Kasumi chose not to kill him in a death-duel he lost. A life-debt to a Lion enemy, witnessed by both armies. She will remember; so must he. The string will pull — in court, in war, or the next time their paths cross.

entries.name: Answering for the broken post — the lost road and taisa Saburō
entries.note: RESOLVED (the dawn reckoning's emotional arc): across one morning Akihiko cured the poisoned company, caught the assassin, turned a Lion column, and brought a witness — and Saburō's cold contempt turned to RELIANCE. He withdrew the Michio comparison ('your grandfather could never finish a thing; you finished three before noon'). The black-sheep blood, vindicated — for now. (Glory 32→36.)

entries.name: The poisoned camp — find the hand killing the Legion (Saburō's charge)
entries.note: Sc.3-5 arc: poison contained, camp cured, assassin Jōji caught — then DEAD under interrogation, the clean proof lost with him. Established: the Lion poisoned the Emperor's Legion to blind it for the western strike (NOT Scorpion). But the evidence is all circumstantial — cured sickness, dead assassin, coded ledger, Sukune's foreknowledge-impression. Saburō's NEW CHARGE: keep pulling the thread quietly/deniably/fast and bring proof a magistrate can't laugh off — before Akodo Shizue (who marked Akihiko) pulls her own threads. To accuse the Lion's Right Hand without proof = suicide.

entries.name: The western strike — the Crane village burning through the gap I left
entries.note: Sc.4 played: Akihiko rode to the smoke with 6 men, found a Lion strike (Akodo Shizue cmdg, Kasumi running down fugitives) gutting a Crane supply-village. He couldn't save it (already lost) — but reframed his demand as courtesy (invoking Kasumi's mercy) + a bluff of incoming Imperial reinforcement, and TURNED THE COLUMN (Water·Courtesy success): Shizue withdrew 'of her own prudence,' sparing the last fugitives. He got no tell from her (she's the prime poison-paymaster but gave nothing provable) and she has MARKED him ('give your uncle my regards'). The Crane war-lifeline is wounded. Akihiko secured witness Daidoji Sukune. Now: carry it all to Saburō + build the case for the Lion's dishonor.
